step_by_step_thinking: Polyether (option D) tends to absorb water over time leading to changes in dimensions, whereas addition silicone (option B) and condensation silicone (option C) do not exhibit significant dimensional change upon storage. Additionally, while polysulfide (option D) maintains some flexibility, it exhibits poorer dimensional stability compared to other elastomeric materials mentioned such as polyether and addition silicone. Given this information, among the listed non-aqueous elastomeric impression materials, polusulfide would likely have the shortest shelf life due to its specific requirements and potential for degradation.
raw_response:
Polyether (option D) tends to absorb water over time leading to changes in dimensions, whereas addition silicone (option B) and condensation silicone (option C) do not exhibit significant dimensional change upon storage. Additionally, while polysulfide (option D) maintains some flexibility, it exhibits poorer dimensional stability compared to other elastomeric materials mentioned such as polyether and addition silicone. Given this information, among the listed non-aqueous elastomeric impression materials, polusulfide would likely have the shortest shelf life due to its specific requirements and potential for degradation.
\boxed{D}
